description: Fix false strict CodeRef preflight failures caused by drift.json reporting 200% drift after a fresh populate because drift comparison mixes absolute and relative file paths.
goal: Make fresh .coderef artifacts pass strict planning when unchanged by normalizing drift comparison to a stable path format.
requirements: Reproduce and document the false 200% drift condition seen after a fresh populate in coderef-workflow.
Normalize current and previous drift comparison element file paths to the same project-relative format before IDs are compared.
Ensure drift.json added, deleted, and modified entries use the normalized path format.
Add a regression test where the previous index uses relative paths and the current scan state uses absolute paths but reports zero drift for unchanged elements.
Preserve the strict 10% drift gate instead of weakening or bypassing strict planning preflight.
out_of_scope: Adding heuristic bypasses in coderef-workflow preflight.
Broad scanner redesign beyond the drift normalization defect.
Renaming MCP tools or weakening the strict planning contract.
constraints: Keep the strict planning contract intact.
Do not add regex or manifest fallback to the strict path.
Keep the fix focused on stable path normalization for drift comparison.
